= AWS RedshiftData
:artifactid: camel-aws2-redshift-starter

Perform operations on AWS Redshift using Redshift Data API.

== What's inside

* xref:{csb-camel-components}::aws2-redshift-data-component.adoc[AWS RedshiftData component], URI syntax: `aws2-redshift-data:label`

Please refer to the above links for usage and configuration details.

== Maven coordinates

[source,xml]
----
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.apache.camel.springboot</groupId>
    <artifactId>camel-aws2-redshift-starter</artifactId>
</dependency>
----

== Spring Boot Auto-Configuration

The starter supports 23 options, which are listed below.

[width="100%",cols="2,5,^1,2",options="header"]
|===
| Name | Description | Default | Type
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.access-key | Amazon AWS Access Key |  | String
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.autowired-enabled | Whether autowiring is enabled. This is used for automatic autowiring options (the option must be marked as autowired) by looking up in the registry to find if there is a single instance of matching type, which then gets configured on the component. This can be used for automatic configuring JDBC data sources, JMS connection factories, AWS Clients, etc. | true | Boolean
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.aws-redshift-data-client | To use an existing configured AwsRedshiftDataClient client. The option is a software.amazon.awssdk.services.redshiftdata.RedshiftDataClient type. |  | RedshiftDataClient
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.configuration | Component configuration. The option is a org.apache.camel.component.aws2.redshift.data.RedshiftData2Configuration type. |  | RedshiftData2Configuration
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.enabled | Whether to enable auto configuration of the aws2-redshift-data component. This is enabled by default. |  | Boolean
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.health-check-consumer-enabled | Used for enabling or disabling all consumer based health checks from this component | true | Boolean
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.health-check-producer-enabled | Used for enabling or disabling all producer based health checks from this component. Notice: Camel has by default disabled all producer based health-checks. You can turn on producer checks globally by setting camel.health.producersEnabled=true. | true | Boolean
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.lazy-start-producer | Whether the producer should be started lazy (on the first message). By starting lazy you can use this to allow CamelContext and routes to startup in situations where a producer may otherwise fail during starting and cause the route to fail being started. By deferring this startup to be lazy then the startup failure can be handled during routing messages via Camel's routing error handlers. Beware that when the first message is processed then creating and starting the producer may take a little time and prolong the total processing time of the processing. | false | Boolean
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.operation | The operation to perform. It can be batchExecuteStatement, cancelStatement, describeStatement, describeTable, executeStatement, getStatementResult, listDatabases, listSchemas, listStatements or listTables |  | RedshiftData2Operations
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.override-endpoint | Set the need for overriding the endpoint. This option needs to be used in combination with the uriEndpointOverride option | false | Boolean
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.pojo-request | If we want to use a POJO request as body or not | false | Boolean
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.profile-credentials-name | If using a profile credentials provider, this parameter will set the profile name |  | String
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.proxy-host | To define a proxy host when instantiating the RedshiftData client |  | String
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.proxy-port | To define a proxy port when instantiating the RedshiftData client |  | Integer
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.proxy-protocol | To define a proxy protocol when instantiating the RedshiftData client | https | Protocol
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.region | The region in which RedshiftData client needs to work. When using this parameter, the configuration will expect the lowercase name of the region (for example, ap-east-1) You'll need to use the name Region.EU_WEST_1.id() |  | String
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.secret-key | Amazon AWS Secret Key |  | String
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.session-token | Amazon AWS Session Token used when the user needs to assume an IAM role |  | String
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.trust-all-certificates | If we want to trust all certificates in case of overriding the endpoint | false | Boolean
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.uri-endpoint-override | Set the overriding uri endpoint. This option needs to be used in combination with overrideEndpoint option |  | String
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.use-default-credentials-provider | Set whether the RedshiftData client should expect to load credentials through a default credentials provider or to expect static credentials to be passed in. | false | Boolean
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.use-profile-credentials-provider | Set whether the RedshiftData client should expect to load credentials through a profile credentials provider. | false | Boolean
| camel.component.aws2-redshift-data.use-session-credentials | Set whether the Redshift client should expect to use Session Credentials. This is useful in a situation in which the user needs to assume an IAM role for doing operations in Redshift. | false | Boolean
|===
